title: Mybench Assessment Claim
description: One signed assessment claim (MYB-10.1, handoff §3). Strict whitelist like every mybench artifact (additionalProperties:false): a claim carries registry identifiers, commitments, bands, and version identifiers — content fields are structurally impossible (privacy invariant #1). Claims stay local until the THREAT_MODEL §3 revision opens a publish gate (invariant #4, MYB-16.2). Patterns use [0-9] (never \d — Unicode digits) and every patterned field is length-pinned because jsonschema matches with re.search, where '$' accepts a trailing newline. The 'no floats' rule cannot be expressed here for the free-form output object; mybench.claims.canonical enforces it on every serialization, and validate_claim runs both checks plus ordering rules the schema cannot express (real-instant timestamps; sorted-unique corpus_commitment arrays).

timestamp.description: Exactly RFC 3339 UTC whole seconds, no fractional part — one canonical encoding per instant (byte-identity rule).

registry_id.description: Descriptor registry entry this claim instantiates (namespace.name). A claim without a registry entry is invalid — the registry (MYB-10.2) is the source of truth for shape, bands, and disclosure.

registry_version.description: Semver of the registry entry the output conforms to; any output-shape change bumps it (handoff §4 rule 6).

scorer.description: The code that computed this claim. Behavior changes bump version (load-bearing, like judge versioning).

measurement.description: null locally; enclave code measurement when attested.

inputs.description: Content-addressed evidence the scorer consumed (handoff §4 rule 5).

corpus_commitment.description: Merkle root(s) over the normalized evidence, lowercase hex. The array form must be sorted ascending and duplicate-free (validate_claim enforces order; uniqueItems here) so identical evidence has exactly one byte representation.

anchor_refs.description: Optional OTS anchor ids relied upon. When present: non-empty, sorted, duplicate-free — 'no anchors' has exactly one representation (key omitted).

output.description: Descriptor-specific payload; bands, not raw values, where §8 requires. Shape is governed by the registry entry (registry_id@registry_version), not by this schema; never empty. No floats — enforced by the canonical serializer.

derivation_class.description: measured = deterministic count/presence/ratio; characterization = taxonomy/archetype assignment, renders at judge-tier styling with confidence (handoff §5).

execution_env.description: Where the scorer ran. Tier presentation for these values is owner-gated (OQ #30) — never mapped to tier names in code.

attestation_evidence.description: MYB-7.18 composite attestation-evidence list; local-unattested claims carry the zero-element case so attested evidence appends later without migration. items:false is deliberate v0 policy: no evidence-item shape is whitelisted yet, so tee-attested claims are structurally unproducible until MYB-7.18 supplies the composite schema — a free-form object here would be a content-smuggling hole in the invariant-#1 whitelist.

signed_at.description: Runner-supplied input timestamp (UTC RFC 3339); scorers never read the clock (handoff §4 rule 2).

signer.description: The verifying key, embedded like anchor batches embed device_pub. kind is SELF-CERTIFIED: it must be bound to a trusted key set (device-binding records / local device key) before any device-tier presentation — see verify_claim. kind=dev is clearly non-production: its claims verify only up to the self-run tier.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature (hex) over the canonical compact sorted JSON of this object WITHOUT the signature field.
